evidence.text: Benefits are amazing but not worth the low salary Great Benefits, Low Salary
RBC Insurance had great products, but that is where it stops. The building had a small gym/exercise room and a cafeteria. Working for RBC Insurance was a demotivating experience.
Bluecross group benefits with great rates, transit reimbursement, free gym membership. Good benefits, culture went downhill
I love the people I work with. The benefits are great Great Benefits and environment
Good benefits, vacation days, good pay Review
Good growth opportunity, especially in Canada Good growth opportunity

RBC is recognized as a top tier financial institution and there is a wide array of services they provide. The benefits are very good and you can learn a lot. Learned a lot about the financial services industry but dissatisfied with the pay and growth opportunities.

Valuable experience: Working as a Relationship Banker allowed me to develop strong communication, sales, and customer service skills. It was rewarding to help clients with their financial goals and offer personalized advice. Team environment: My colleagues were supportive and knowledgeable, creating a collaborative atmosphere in the branch. There was always someone willing to offer guidance or share their expertise....

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior IT Risk Analyst
evidence.text: Good leadership in my business line Opportunity for Growth
great benefits, flexible, great teams to work in Enjoyable with minimal stress and opportunity to learn lots of new apps and tools

Relatively relaxed work environment (on the trade floor anyway), incredibly friendly work environment, helpful coworkers, opportunities for gaining licensure (series 7 & 66/63) are fairly common Great place to start a career with opportunity to move up

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Registered Client Service Associate
evidence.text: Friendly work environment but can be very cliquey. Client service associate
Small but large enough to have proficient systems and support in back and front office. Very inclusive and benefits are incredible. 24 weeks maternity leave. Inclusive

evidence.role: Former employee - Help Desk/Desktop Support Engineer
evidence.text: good place, Did Migration Support and Desktop support Desktop Support Engineer
Friendly employees, good culture, social events Job review
Work life balance depends on the group you are in, but was very positive for me Work/Life Balance
Smaller deal teams encourage direct collaboration with Senior team members Great place to start a career, but work-life balance will be heavily impacted

Work/life balance, competitive compensation, company values, high integrity within the financial services industry, opportunity for personal growth, accessible corporate structure This is a company that compensates employees well with a good work/life balance.

Great Place to Work: RBC Bank offers a positive work environment with supportive colleagues and opportunities for professional growth. Competitive Compensation: The bank provides competitive salaries and benefits, ensuring employees feel valued and well-compensated for their efforts. Professional Development: RBC Bank invests in employee development with various training programs and opportunities for career advance...
Good company for self-starters overall Poor work-life balance
Some of the groups are competent and team-oriented, such as sales and trading. Run - do not apply for banking

I interned at RBC Wealth Management in Minneapolis, MN as a business analyst. I was in the Technical and Operations program (T&O). I worked in the Project Delivery Office (PDO). Great team and nice people for the most part. Learned great life lessons and made some great connections. No loyalty and you will only hear crickets
great group of people to work with Very comfortable environment to work in, the people are wonderful and there is no ego at RBC.
Job security. Thereâs a lot of work to be done Mid experience
Benefits, office space, job security Management is mediocre

evidence.role: Current employee - Associate II - Bond Payments
evidence.text: Low pay compared to industry. Good Company low pay

The job is a low paying job but management keep giving you their work. Thus, you are overworked with the lowest pay in the company. There is also sexism in the environment. The leader of this specific branch does not know how to communicate especially with people of color. lack of leadership. I do not recommend this company to any woman and people of color ( who are limited anyway) Very low pay

Their products available to sell were limited. Part of this was due to them being on the market to be sold. RBC had banks in the Southeast and had purchased Liberty Life Insurance Company as their insurance arm in 2000 and then in 2010 RBC sold Liberty Life and has completely pulled out of the United States. They no longer exist
Low pay Hybrid - currently 2 days/week but moving to 4 days per week in September FTE Review
Stressful work environment and long hours Great Job
Bad Middle Management. No career growth. Expected to work overtime and get no recognition. Badly Managed, Over Worked Employees
Don't hold your hand when it comes to training. Can be overwhelming if new to the industry. Not a lot of promotion opportunity. Aggressive first finance job

Incoherent policies that seem to change every 6 months. Employees are out for themselves and managers are absent or non-existent. Your career path is based on who you know and not on your talent. The inclusiveness and progressiveness of the Canadian head office does not seem to apply to the US entities. Witnessed many episodes of discriminatory behavior against minorities and homophobia which were never addressed by...
No room for advancement or promotion Very Good Place to Work

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Credit Risk Review Officer
evidence.text: No growth or promotion opportunities Team leaders are poor managers with one extremely inexperienced and the other incompetent Great work environment, no job mobility
Poor technology, no work life balance Itâs aight
Not that diverse on the employee level Great work-life balance
bureaucratic data systems are not the best Great Culture
No work life balance but maybe thatâs just me Analyst
Facilities not organized. Not a lot of employee perks Vp
But management people were playing politics Good Environment But Discrimination exist also
Angry managers, unrealistic expectations, unethical decisions Toxic environment
Bogged down by internal politics Smart people, but bogged down by internal politics

Senior management often did not seem to trust or empower the employees to make decisions that were necessary to properly perform their responsibilities. I certainly learned a lot in my time at RBC, but felt that management did not trust/empower employees to make decisions.
Too many re-org; some managers are incompetent and rude. Office politics
401k does not vest immediately Friendly environment
Some members didn't seem to go above and beyond Kind people, solid office
not really any cons here Great Community

evidence.role: Current employee - Associate, Global Investment Banking
evidence.text: I believe this is more of an industry con, but too many books are done, some of which are not even required in the first place. Specifically to RBC, it is probably the best time to take lead and move officially into bulge bracket. Exciting, promising, and very inclusive.

Compensation of back office employees not on par with market rates. A lot of oversight from Canadian leaders, at times do not have ability to innovate as necessary. Culture is OK, some groups have better, not consistent, depends on business line. Lean teams, no support to add headcount when workloads are high. Some good, some bad
